name: Olympia
shortDescription: Olympia was a sanctuary of ancient Greece in Elis, known for being the site of the ancient Olympic Games. It was dedicated to Zeus and other Olympian deities.
description: Olympia, located in a valley in Elis in the Peloponnese, Greece, was a major Panhellenic religious sanctuary. From the 10th century BC, it was a center for the worship of Zeus, the king of the gods, and Hera, his consort. The site's significance stemmed primarily from its hosting of the ancient Olympic Games, held every four years from 776 BC to 393 AD. These games were a crucial part of Greek identity and served as a period of truce and unity among the often-warring city-states. The sanctuary included numerous temples, altars, treasuries, and athletic structures, like the Stadium and Palaestra, showcasing both religious devotion and athletic prowess. Myths surrounding Olympia often involve Pelops and Oenomaus, where Pelops won Hippodamia, the daughter of Oenomaus, in a chariot race, thereby founding the Olympic Games. The site's association with Zeus is underscored by the massive Temple of Zeus, which housed a colossal gold and ivory statue of the god, considered one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World. The olive tree, from which victors' wreaths were made, was also profoundly associated with Olympia.

location.cosmological: Located in the western Peloponnese region of Greece, in the valley of Elis, near the confluence of the Alfeios and Kladeos rivers.

sources.title: Olympia: Cult, Sport, and Ancient Festival
sources.author: W. Decker and M. Herb
